text:
September 1st: Part 4 (9④, Kugatsu Tsuitachi (Yon)) is the 75 th chapter of the Hunter × Hunter manga, written and illustrated by Yoshihiro Togashi.

Synopsis

Several Mafia members spot the Phantom Troupe's hot-air balloon floating away from the city toward the Gordeau Desert. One of them is surprised that it's not an airship and wonders where the missing guests are. Another guesses that the Troupe's plan was more than just an armored heist, telling the others to contact the Dons so they can call in the Shadow Beasts.

After tracking and shooting down the balloon, dozens of Mafia members stand in a large canyon and yell for the six Troupe members to come down from the ledge above. Shalnark is happy to see so many, and Uvogin tells the others that he will take care of all the Mafia members by himself, sliding down the cliff to face them with guns drawn.

One Mafia member approaches Uvogin and points a gun right at his face, asking if he kidnapped the guests. Uvogin confirms it, and the Mafia member asks if he thought that he would get away with it, wondering who his leader is. After Uvogin just smirks in reply and the man shoots, it's shown that Uvogin caught the bullet with his teeth. Uvogin then breaks the man's neck with a single swipe, leaving the others in shock and telling himself that he wanted to be stronger than anything.

As Uvogin grabs the man by his head and begins attacking the nearest Mafia members, the other Troupe members comment on how easily Uvogin will kill them, wondering why they keep trying to fight him. With several Mafia members dead, Uvogin says that it won't even count as a warm-up before the Shadow Beasts arrive. At that moment, a sniper fires and shoots Uvogin in the side of the head, but it only causes him some pain since he surrounded his body with aura. He spots the two snipers in the distance and says that he hates sneaky cowards, picking up two rocks and hurling them at the snipers.

After both rocks hit their mark and kill the snipers, a Mafia member approaches with a rocket launcher and says that he will easily blow him up since the weapon can take out a tank. As Uvogin feels slighted being compared to just a tank, the man fires the weapon directly at him, causing an enormous explosion which Kurapika and Basho see as they drive up to the scene. The Mafia members are confident that they killed him, but when the smoke clears, Uvogin himself appears mostly unscathed and says that the attack hurt, with the clothes on his torso burned off and some areas on his body still smoking.

The Mafia members scream in fear and begin to run away, but Uvogin chuckles and says that they have no chance of escaping. In the distance, Kurapika and the others wonder what caused the explosion. Looking through binoculars, Kurapika spots Uvogin mercilessly beheading and bisecting the Mafia members, confirming that he uses Nen and is incredibly strong. He adds that guns are useless against him, handing the binoculars to Dalzollene so that he can see the carnage for himself.

Using a monocular to see the massacre, a frightened Squala refuses to fight and tells the others that the man is ripping his victims apart with his bare hands. As Dalzollene says that they have to try at least, Melody senses another heartbeat and something begins to approach the group by moving just below the ground. Kurapika and Basho jump back before a disturbing-looking person emerges and introduces himself as Worm, one of the Shadow Beasts. He asks them what family the group is from, and Dalzollene answers the Ritz Family and that they are acting as bodyguards for Mr. Nostrade. Worm tells him that they should stay away from the fight, and three more Shadow Beasts suddenly appear, calling themselves experts and calmly approaching Uvogin (with Worm burrowing back under the ground).

As the other Troupe members play a game of cards, Feitan notices the three approaching and Uvogin guesses that they are the Shadow Beasts, asking them where they took the auction items. Rabid Dog in turn asks Uvogin what he did with the guests, and Uvogin calmly answers that they killed them, asking him again where the items are. As Uvogin begins to threaten the three, Worm suddenly appears behind him and punches him hard in the face. Although the punch does damage, Worm's hand is broken in the process and after praising his strike, Uvogin returns one of his own, knocking out several of Worm's teeth and popping out one of his eyes as the other Troupe members continue playing their game of cards.
